title: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
summary: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickly.

title: Supreme Court Rejects Colorado Law Banning ‘Conversion Therapy’ for L.G.B.T.Q. Minors
summary: The state and more than 20 others restrict therapists from trying to change the gender identity or sexual orientation of clients under 18.

title: How Immigration Policies Like Birthright Citizenship Shaped the Supreme Court Justices’ Histories
summary: As the justices prepare to hear a landmark case about birthright citizenship, their family stories are a reminder that the law has shaped who can be an American.

title: U.S. Gas Prices Hit $4 a Gallon on Average, a ‘Headache’ for Drivers and Trump
summary: A month since the first U.S.-Israeli attacks and Iran’s response effectively shut off Persian Gulf oil, drivers are paying significantly more to fill up.

title: Will $4 Gas Hurt Trump’s Approval Ratings? Here’s What History Shows.
summary: Presidents since at least the Carter administration have seen their approval ratings tied to gas prices. But there are signs the correlation may be weakening.

title: Pentagon Weighs Using Anti-Drone Lasers Near D.C. Military Base
summary: Sightings of drones around Fort McNair, the Army base where Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Secretary of State Marco Rubio live, have prompted consideration of deploying the new technology.

title: ‘The Book of Mormon’ Is Sorry if You Were Offended for 15 Years
summary: The taboo-busting, gasp-inducing Broadway musical comedy has been a hit with audiences and critics. But could it be produced today?

title: ‘God Squad’ Waives Environmental Rules for Drilling in the Gulf of Mexico
summary: The panel voted to override Endangered Species Act restrictions on oil and gas activities in the Gulf of Mexico, home to critically endangered whales and other imperiled wildlife.

title: A New Jersey Teen Finds Treasure, and More, in Abandoned Storage Units
summary: Michael Haskell, 17, set out to make some money from his locker dives. He ended up learning about life.

title: Putin’s Internet Blackout: A Chaotic Drive to Cut Off Russians From the World
summary: With new outages and blockages, President Vladimir V. Putin is taking his boldest steps yet to control Russians’ communications.

title: Cesar Chavez Was a Voice for Mexican Americans Like Me. Now, We Grieve.
summary: The United Farm Workers co-founder had been celebrated as an exemplar of civil rights. Then, a Times investigation found extensive evidence of his abuse of women.

title: On What Was Once Chavez Day, Some Try to Highlight a Movement, Not a Man
summary: The reconsideration of the legacy of Cesar Chavez after he was accused of abusing women and girls has led some to question the lionization of icons, when reality is so often a letdown.

title: How Cesar Chavez Abused His Power
summary: The civil rights icon had a history of sexually abusing women and girls, which the Times reporters Manny Fernandez and Sarah Hurtes spent five years investigating. They spoke to “The Daily” about how they uncovered the story.

title: In a Moment of Division, the Astronauts on the Artemis II Mission Hope to Inspire
summary: Can the four astronauts of the NASA mission Artemis II make a difference in a distracted and divided world?

title: Should Rich New Yorkers Get Free Stuff, Too? Mamdani Says Yes.
summary: A free preschool center in one of the city’s wealthiest neighborhoods raises questions about Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s vow to expand universal child care.

title: Zohran Mamdani Puts New York City Back on TikTok
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is lifting the ban on governmental use of the social media app, one of a handful that the mayor used to amplify his campaign messaging.

title: How Instagram’s ‘PG-13’ Branding for Teens Unraveled
summary: The social media giant, under legal pressure from the Motion Picture Association, has retreated from its use of the movie rating in its marketing.

title: Four Tied to N.Y.C. Shelter Contractor Charged in Corruption Inquiry
summary: The arrests were part of an investigation that is also examining the conduct of a city councilwoman, her sister and the Brooklyn Democratic leader’s husband.

title: She Was a Famous Millennial Feminist. Her Polyamory Memoir Is Heartbreaking.
summary: Almost every ideology can be wielded to make women feel that they’re failing.

title: The People Trump Pardoned Are on a Crime Spree
summary: The president and the Republican Party deserve to pay a political price for the pardons.

title: A Lifeline for Cuba
summary: We look at the energy crisis on the Caribbean island.

title: Don’t Know Where to Go This Summer? Let Us Help!
summary: We’re responding to readers’ travel quandaries with our suggestions.

title: U.S. Assures Colombian President Petro He Does Not Face Charges Right Now
summary: The Justice Department is examining Gustavo Petro for possible ties to drug traffickers. U.S. officials have told him that for now he does not face criminal charges arising from the probes.

title: McCormick to Combine With Unilever Food Unit to Create a Spice Giant
summary: The deal ends Unilever’s time in the food industry and will create a multibillion-dollar group under the McCormick name.

title: FDA Is Expected to Lift Restriction on Peptides, Heeding RFK Jr.’s Wishes
summary: The peptides, which are increasingly marketed as providing longevity and health benefits, were removed in 2023 from the agency’s list of products that compounding pharmacies can sell.
